Alerts & notifications
Critical — act now
DATP Public Comment reading room is live. The City of Colorado Springs Digital Accessibility Transition Plan (Draft 2026), with the full plan embedded as a PDF and twelve structured public comments residents can adapt and submit. Comment portal links live in the reading room.
Public comment · COS Digital Accessibility Transition Plan · May 2026
Open the DATP reading room →
BOCC petition — service plan transparency & algorithmic pricing disclosure. A general resident petition with four narrow asks within existing BOCC authority. Sign and send.
Civic action · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
Open petition letter →
Veterans' petition to the EPC BOCC. Companion petition for veterans of any branch — service plan transparency and algorithmic pricing protections, framed around military housing harms.
Civic action · Veterans · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
Open veterans' petition →
LDC update — public comment on service plan transparency. A 300-word comment ready to submit through the El Paso County Land Development Code update process.
Public comment · LDC update · El Paso County · May 2026
Open LDC comment →
Background brief — algorithmic rent pricing in El Paso County. Sourced reference for journalists, policy analysts, and the COS Chamber housing committee: federal antitrust action, local policy options, and the case for BOCC-level service plan transparency.
Reference · Housing & pricing · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
Read background brief →
HOME-ARP comment window is live — closes May 26, 2026. COS is collecting public comment on the HOME-ARP Allocation Plan amendment via an ArcGIS Survey123 form Esri's own ACR rates as "Partially Supports" on WCAG 2.1 A/AA. A WCAG 2.1 AA Drupal Webform replacement is ready to install — bilingual EN/ES, JSON webhook + staff email + native storage, inline accommodation intake.
24 CFR 91.105 · ADA Title II · May 4, 2026
Open the accessible HTML form preview → Open the HOME-ARP bundle README →
Downtown Funding and Records Search Search BID assessments, DDA grants, TIF reimbursements, board meetings, and financial records in one place.
Annual adopted budget — C.R.S. 29-1-101 et seq · April 10, 2026
Open the search portal →
ADA Title II deadline: April 24, 2026 — 16 days. All local government websites must meet WCAG 2.1 AA. Zero district sites are confirmed compliant on PDF documents.
HB21-1110 · 28 C.F.R. Part 35 · April 8, 2026
Open district audit dashboard →
249 of 284 metro districts have no website. CORA request is the only way a resident can access their own district's records.
EPC Assessor audit · April 2026
See district audit →
WS Districts (WSDM) serves 30+ districts on a single WordPress site. Last calendar entry: 2021. No governance docs. No financials. One compliance letter fixes all 30+ simultaneously.
Platform audit · April 2026
Draft compliance letter →
High priority
All posted PDFs likely fail WCAG 1.3.1. Meeting minutes, budgets, and audit docs are almost certainly untagged. This is the real HB21-1110 failure — not the homepage.
ADA document audit · April 2026
View ADA analysis →
0 of 8 audited sites post their service plan. C.R.S. §32-1-202 requires it. This is the foundational legal document. Not one district links it publicly.
Governance gap · §32-1-202
See audit dashboard →
Annual audit documents: publicly posted by 0 of 8 districts. Required by §32-1-106. Due to State Auditor by July 31. The clock is running.
Governance gap · §32-1-106 · due July 31
See audit dashboard →
Informational
2 CORA requests pending response. Military housing numbers and district governance records both awaiting reply. Response window is still open.
CORA tracker · April 2026
View tracker →
Wolfersberger compliance and Bellamese analysis complete. New findings ready for review on developer agreements, compliance history, and parcel-level records.
Compliance analysis · April 2026
Read analysis →
Jurisdiction finder is live. Enter any El Paso County address to find your metro districts, school district, city jurisdiction, and utilities — plain language, no login.
New · April 2026
Find my jurisdiction →
CSPD Blotter — Weekly History is live. 271 incidents pulled directly from the live blotter feed, paired with a Poisson 95% confidence band to flag statistical anomalies week to week. Tabs are full APG-pattern keyboard accessible; every chart has an aria-label.
New · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open blotter dashboard →
Lorson Ranch financial dashboard is live. Seven cross-pledged metro districts, $94.2M aggregate audited deficit, IGA money flows, and a per-home tax calculator — sourced from 2026 budgets and 2024 audits. Companion plain-language briefing available as a download.
New · Special Districts · April 2026
Open Lorson Ranch dashboard →
CBI Missing Persons form — accessible rebuild is live. Resolves all seven critical/serious findings from the ADA audit: skip link, live regions, focus management, EN/ES localization, real-time validation messaging. Drops into the existing Drupal Webform infrastructure with optional Claude API routing.
New · State · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open CBI form rebuild →
LexisNexis ADAM Program dashboard — accessible rebuild is live. Audit and reference rebuild of the missing-children dashboard: APG-pattern tabs with roving tabindex and full keyboard nav, solid amber focus rings (3:1 against any surface), darker success-green for AA contrast, and "View Poster" placeholders converted from dead anchors to real buttons.
New · Private Sector · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open ADAM dashboard →

Tier 1 · Where you live

Local — El Paso County & Colorado Springs

Everything that touches your address day to day — city services, county offices, downtown business records, the metro and special districts billed to your property, and the cross-jurisdictional tools that pull from all of them. Start here if you're not sure where to look. See the full org chart →

Start here

Find what governs your address

Routes to the jurisdiction finder with your input. Returns city, county, school district, metro districts, and utilities for that address.

Active right now — front-and-center initiatives

County · BOCC petition

Service plan transparency & algorithmic pricing disclosure

Open window · sign and send

City · DATP comment

Digital Accessibility Transition Plan — public comment reading room

Open window · 12 structured comments

City · HOME-ARP

HOME-ARP Allocation Plan — accessible comment form

Closes May 26, 2026 · EN/ES

Shared — start here

Shared · You

Find my jurisdiction

Type your address. Know immediately which governments touch it — city, county, school district, metro districts, utilities. The entry point for everything else.

Live ↗

Shared · PPROEM · Emergency

Pikes Peak Regional Alerts

Live regional alerts for El Paso County. Type an address — see whether it's inside an active evacuation, warning, or advisory zone. Reads PPROEM's hazard feed directly; subscribe routes to Peak Alerts.

Live ↗

Shared · PPROEM · Policy

Pikes Peak Regional Emergency Operations Plan

The 2026 all-hazards plan adopted by El Paso County and the City of Colorado Springs. 14 sections, hazard risk by jurisdiction, all 28 ESFs, ECC activation levels. The full document, readable in a browser.

Live ↗

City · Community Resources

Helping Hands — El Paso County resource finder

A searchable, ranked index of ~200 community resources — food, shelter, crisis lines, medical, legal, jobs, immigration, addiction. Type plain English ("kids are hungry") and the page surfaces the fastest help first. Adapted from the PPLD Helping Hands directory.

Live ↗

City · Forestry

Trees for Colorado Springs

A merged, filterable reference for trees that thrive at 6,000–7,500 ft. Combines the city's Approved Street Tree List with curated fruit and native options. Search by name, fruit, size, or what they feed.

Live ↗

Shared · State · CDPHE

El Paso County Mortality Dashboard

EPC death data compared to Colorado and US averages, with dedicated Fort Carson and military installation analysis. Data: CDPHE CoHID + CDC NCHS + DoD. An accessible redesign of the state tool.

Live ↗

Shared · 284 metro districts

EPC Metro District Audit Dashboard

Governance gaps, ADA compliance, and platform analysis across all 284 El Paso County metro districts. 88% have zero public web presence. The data, laid bare.

9 alerts ↗

Shared · Citizen action

Metro District Citizen Toolkit

Sample CORA request letters, BOCC templates, DOJ complaint drafts, every contact you need. Somewhere to send the knowledge once you have it.

In Progress ↗

Shared · CORA · Open records

CORA Requests Tracker & Military Housing

Live tracker for all open records requests — who was asked, what came back. Military housing figures cross-referenced with public and survey data. Two requests pending. Clock is running.

2 Pending ↗

Shared · Developer compliance

Wolfersberger Compliance & Bellamese Analysis

Developer agreement compliance history, parcel-level records, and findings from the Bellamese analysis. Months of records requests, assembled.

In Progress ↗

City of Colorado Springs

City · ADA / Accessibility

Digital Accessibility Transition Plan — open reading room

The City of Colorado Springs Digital Accessibility Transition Plan (Draft 2026), as an accessible reading room — full plan embedded as PDF, with twelve structured public comments residents can adapt and submit. Active public-comment window.

City · Public Records

COS CORA — Form & Implementation

Colorado's open-records law is legally binding. The city's request form, paired with a plain-language guide on how the city is supposed to follow it — and what to do when they creatively interpret that obligation.

Colorado Springs CORA Form
Submit a request
↗
CORA Implementation Guide
How the city should follow the law
↗

City · Traffic & Safety

Traffic Safety, Signal Timing & Red Light Data

Crash records, FHWA funding analysis, intersection signal-timing action tools, and red-light camera citation data across 20 Colorado Springs intersections.

Signal Timing Action Dashboard

City · Police

Colorado Springs Police Department

Records request redesign and a resident-first reorganization of CSPD data — answering the questions people actually ask about their neighborhoods.

CSPD Records Request — Redesign
Police reports · 911 audio · body-cam · background checks
↗
What Residents Actually Ask
CSPD data, reorganized around five real questions
↗

CSPD Field Entry — Crash Report

City · goGov · ADA Audit

goGov Platform — Accessibility Audit

Accessibility audit of the goGov platform used by the City of Colorado Springs. Full findings in PDF.

Live ↗

City & County · Contact Portal · Prototype

Contact the City — Rebuild Prototype

A working rebuild of the GovOutreach contact portal, plus permits, CORA, and El Paso County Environmental Health — 119 topics across 13 groups and 3 jurisdictions. Four ways to find what you need: plain-language search, by intent, by office, and scenario journeys ("building a home," "opening a restaurant"). Dynamic per-topic forms, real map picker, accessible image upload, ADA footer, jurisdiction badges. 0 WCAG 2.1 AA violations under axe.

Prototype ↗

City · Housing · HOME-ARP · LIVE comment window

HOME-ARP Comment Form — Drupal Webform Replacement

Replacement for the ArcGIS Survey123 form the City uses to collect public comments on the HOME-ARP Allocation Plan amendment. Esri's own ACR rates Survey123 as "Partially Supports" on multiple WCAG 2.1 Level A and AA criteria — not acceptable for a 30-day federally-regulated participation window under 24 CFR 91.105. This bundle ships the form WCAG 2.1 AA accessible by default, bilingual EN/ES, with three parallel output channels (JSON webhook, staff email, native Drupal storage) and an inline accommodation request that routes to the Office of Accessibility. Comment window: April 24 – May 26, 2026.

Bundle README
What this delivers · compliance posture · platform integration
↗
Install Guide
30–45 minute Drupal admin walkthrough
↗
Bilingual Pattern (EN/ES)
Reusable language switcher for any city form
↗
COS Digital Accessibility Audit (.docx)
Findings the rebuild resolves
↓
home_arp_form.zip
Packaged Drupal Webform bundle, ready to import
↓

El Paso County

County · Assessor

Assessor — Property Conveyances

Who owns what, and when did that change? Property transfer records, decoded for humans. Know your neighborhood before your realtor does.

In Progress ↗

County · Land Development Code

Land Development Code

A readable entry point into the county's development code. Because someone has to be reachable when the code does something confusing (so, constantly).

LDC — Contact & Navigation
Who to reach at the county
↗
LDC — Accessibility Statement
WCAG posture & remediation
↗

County · Population Dashboard

El Paso Population Components

Births, deaths, and migration — the components that actually move a county's population numbers, broken down for residents rather than demographers.

In Progress ↗

County · Utilities

CSU Residential Water Accounts Dashboard

Colorado Springs Utilities residential water account data, 2005 to 2026 year-to-date — produced in response to CORA request W001936-041726.

Live ↗

County · Sheriff

El Paso County Public Safety Dashboard

Sheriff's office data, crime mapping, and public safety trends across El Paso County — compiled from public records and agency sources.

Live ↗

County · CitizenConnect

Citizen Connect — El Paso County

ADA audit of the county's citizen portal plus a full redesign proposal — because telling the county what you need shouldn't require a manual.

Citizen Connect — ADA Audit
1 serious · 3 moderate · 5 minor · 44 passing
↗
City Permits, Licenses & Records

County · BOCC

Board of County Commissioners — petitions & comments

Ready-to-send petitions, a public comment for the LDC update, and a sourced background brief on algorithmic rent pricing. Four narrow asks within existing BOCC authority.

Resident petition — service plan transparency
General petition · algorithmic pricing disclosure
↗
Veterans' petition to the BOCC
Any branch · military housing harms framing

LDC update — public comment
300 words · ready to submit
↗
Algorithmic rent pricing — background brief
Sourced reference for press & policy analysts
↗

County · Public Works

Road & Bridge — ADA Audit & Remediation Bundle

Manual WCAG 2.1 AA audit of publicworks.elpasoco.com/road-bridge/ paired with a deployable WordPress mu-plugin that patches the runtime-fixable findings — built for the county's actual stack (The7 + WPBakery + Slider Revolution + Gravity Forms). Live overlay-tested in Chrome; no console errors, no visual regressions.

Remediation Checklist
Owner-keyed · 20 findings · printable
↗
Original Audit Document
May 6, 2026 · 8 critical · 6 serious · 5 moderate
↓
Delivery Bundle
WordPress plugin zip + PDF checklist + audit · 44 KB
↓

County · Infrastructure

Strategic Performance & Property Accountability

El Paso County's strategic-plan KPIs (infrastructure, service quality, community trust, health & safety) and a parcel-level view of what the county tracks (and doesn't) about the road in front of your property. Roads dataset · sample parcel pre-loaded.

Strategic Performance Dashboard
Pillar KPIs · cross-department accountability
↗
Property Accountability — parcel lookup
Enter your schedule number · what the county tracks
↗
KPI Detail
Methodology · sub-metrics · related actions
↗

Downtown Colorado Springs

Downtown · Full Disclosure

Downtown Colorado Springs — Records & Businesses

The first searchable index for downtown money and the businesses that receive it. Paired with a directory of every business inside the BID footprint.

Downtown Funding & Records Search
BID · DDA · TIF · board meetings
↗
Downtown Business Directory
Every business in the BID footprint
↗

Special Districts

Special Districts · Lorson Ranch MDs 1–7

Lorson Ranch Metropolitan Districts

Seven cross-pledged metro districts financing one community. Mill levies, bond debt, IGA money flows, audited net position, and a tax calculator — sourced from 2026 budgets and 2024 audits. $94.2M aggregate deficit, in plain language.

Financial Dashboard
Interactive · mill levies, bonds, IGAs, calculator
↗
Briefing (.docx)
Companion narrative · full plain-language walk-through
↓

Special Districts · Teller 911 · CORA starter

The Unsung Heroes — A CORA Project Anyone Can Do

A semester-length public records project that produces real findings about how El Paso County supports its 911 dispatchers. Three records requests. Three agencies. One ratio that tells you whether the support matches the load. Whatever the answer, it belongs in the public record.

In Progress ↗

What comes next

Next, see how Colorado state agencies connect into this work — CCRD and DOJ Civil Rights as parallel ADA paths, DOLA's special-district registry under CRS Title 32, CDPHE's mortality and environmental data on El Paso County.

Get email updates

We'll email you a plain-language summary with links to the source documents whenever this office posts new public-record material.

By subscribing you agree to receive emails about this topic. We'll send a confirmation link first. Unsubscribe any time. (consent v2026-04-30-v1)
